title: Digital & Technology Transformation
transformationType: Rewiring how work gets done through digital tools, data, and automation.
statement: We are transforming from manual, fragmented, and reactive processes to digital, integrated, and intelligence-enabled workflows so that decisions and services improve continuously.

examples.label: From paper forms and PDFs to end-to-end digital journeys
examples.exemplar: Government Digital Service redesigned services like tax and benefits around complete user journeys, eliminating paper and duplicate steps.

examples.label: From legacy IT owned by departments to shared digital platforms
examples.exemplar: Service NSW built a whole-of-government platform used by dozens of agencies to deliver services consistently.

examples.label: From static reports to real-time operational dashboards
examples.exemplar: New Zealand Ministry of Health uses near-real-time data during system stress (e.g., COVID response) to inform operational decisions.

examples.label: From staff hunting for information to systems that proactively surface what matters
examples.exemplar: Netflix internal platforms push actionable insights directly to teams rather than relying on manual analysis.

designProvocation: Where does work still exist purely because the system cannot 'see' itself?

title: Operating Model Transformation
transformationType: Redesigning how authority, work, and accountability are organized.
statement: We are transforming from siloed, function-based structures to mission-oriented, cross-functional teams so that outcomes—not outputs—drive how we work.

examples.label: From departments optimizing mandates to shared missions
examples.exemplar: Government of Finland has experimented with phenomenon-based budgeting and cross-ministerial missions.

examples.label: From annual plans to rolling priorities
examples.exemplar: Spotify popularized squad- and tribe-based planning with frequent reprioritization.

examples.label: From escalation-heavy governance to clear decision rights at the edge
examples.exemplar: Buurtzorg empowers self-managing teams with minimal hierarchy.

examples.label: From project handoffs to persistent product teams
examples.exemplar: US Digital Service embeds durable teams within agencies rather than short-term projects.

designProvocation: Where does the org chart prevent the work from flowing?

title: Culture & Leadership Transformation
transformationType: Shifting norms, incentives, and leadership behaviours.
statement: We are transforming from risk-averse, compliance-driven culture to one that values learning, trust, and shared responsibility so that people can act with confidence.

examples.label: From 'don't fail' to learning cultures
examples.exemplar: NASA institutionalized after-action reviews following failures like Challenger and Columbia.

examples.label: From heroic leaders to collective leadership
examples.exemplar: Haier shifted to micro-enterprises with distributed leadership.

examples.label: From permission-seeking to intent-based autonomy
examples.exemplar: US Navy adopted intent-based leadership practices championed by David Marquet.

examples.label: From blame to reflection after action
examples.exemplar: Toyota uses structured reflection (hansei) as a core practice.

designProvocation: What signals does leadership send when things go wrong?

title: Customer / Citizen Experience (CX) Transformation
transformationType: Designing around lived experience rather than organizational convenience.
statement: We are transforming from services designed around internal structures to experiences designed around people's real needs and life events.

examples.label: From multiple applications to one journey across programs
examples.exemplar: Service Canada has moved toward bundled services around life events like retirement.

examples.label: From 'contact us' to anticipatory services
examples.exemplar: Estonia provides automatic child benefits triggered by birth registration.

examples.label: From channel teams to journey owners
examples.exemplar: Capital One assigns ownership to customer journeys rather than channels.

examples.label: From policy-first to experience-informed policy
examples.exemplar: MindLab embedded user research directly into policymaking.

designProvocation: Where do people experience your organization as a maze?

title: Workforce & Talent Transformation
transformationType: Rethinking roles, skills, and capability building.
statement: We are transforming from static roles and job descriptions to dynamic, skills-based models so that talent can flow to where it is most needed.

examples.label: From fixed positions to talent pools
examples.exemplar: Unilever uses an internal talent marketplace to match skills to work.

examples.label: From training events to learning in the flow of work
examples.exemplar: IBM embeds continuous learning platforms into daily work.

examples.label: From consultants to internal capability building
examples.exemplar: UK NHS built internal improvement academies rather than outsourcing change.

examples.label: From credentials to demonstrated skills
examples.exemplar: Google reduced degree requirements in favor of skill-based hiring.

designProvocation: Where is talent underused because the system cannot see it?

title: Policy & Governance Transformation
transformationType: Changing rules, incentives, and decision logic.
statement: We are transforming from rules-driven, output-focused policy to outcomes-oriented, adaptive governance so that policy evolves with reality.

examples.label: From prescriptive regulation to principle-based policy
examples.exemplar: Financial Conduct Authority moved toward outcomes-focused regulation.

examples.label: From closed cycles to iterative policy
examples.exemplar: Behavioural Insights Team uses experimentation to refine policy continuously.

examples.label: From mandates to shared missions
examples.exemplar: European Commission adopted mission-oriented innovation frameworks.

examples.label: From compliance to outcome accountability
examples.exemplar: New Zealand Treasury introduced wellbeing budgeting.

designProvocation: Which policies are frozen snapshots of a world that no longer exists?

title: Service System Integration
transformationType: Aligning multiple organizations around shared outcomes.
statement: We are transforming from fragmented services and handoffs to integrated pathways with shared accountability for outcomes.

examples.label: From referrals to integrated pathways
examples.exemplar: Kaiser Permanente integrates care delivery, financing, and prevention.

examples.label: From boundaries to place-based models
examples.exemplar: Greater Manchester Combined Authority integrated health and social care at the regional level.

examples.label: From crisis to prevention
examples.exemplar: Housing First prioritizes stable housing before crisis intervention.

examples.label: From 'not our mandate' to shared outcomes
examples.exemplar: Ontario Health Teams align multiple providers around population outcomes.

designProvocation: Where does the system force people to repeat their story?

title: Funding & Resource Flow Transformation
transformationType: Aligning money with learning and outcomes.
statement: We are transforming from rigid, line-item funding to flexible, mission-aligned resource flows so that learning and impact can scale.

examples.label: From annual budgets to multi-year funding
examples.exemplar: Social Impact Bonds fund long-term outcomes rather than activities.

examples.label: From program silos to pooled funding
examples.exemplar: Better Care Fund pools health and social care budgets.

examples.label: From pilots to scaling pathways
examples.exemplar: UNDP Accelerator Labs explicitly designs pathways from experiment to scale.

examples.label: From spend tracking to value measurement
examples.exemplar: OECD promotes value-for-money and outcomes frameworks.

designProvocation: What promising work dies because funding cannot move?

title: Data & Learning System Transformation
transformationType: Making learning a core system capability.
statement: We are transforming from retrospective reporting to real-time learning systems so that the organization can sense, adapt, and improve continuously.

examples.label: From lagging indicators to leading signals
examples.exemplar: Johns Hopkins Health System uses early warning indicators for patient safety.

examples.label: From KPIs to learning questions
examples.exemplar: IDEO frames metrics as learning hypotheses.

examples.label: From data ownership to sharing ecosystems
examples.exemplar: Barcelona City Council built open data and data commons models.

examples.label: From evaluation at the end to continuous learning
examples.exemplar: World Bank integrates adaptive learning into projects.

designProvocation: What would change if metrics were tools for inquiry, not judgment?

title: Ecosystem & Partnership Transformation
transformationType: Shifting from solo delivery to orchestration.
statement: We are transforming from acting as a single provider to orchestrating an ecosystem of partners so that complex challenges can be addressed together.

examples.label: From contracts to long-term partnerships
examples.exemplar: NHS England uses alliance contracting for integrated care.

examples.label: From competition to collaboration
examples.exemplar: C40 Cities coordinates cities to act collectively on climate.

examples.label: From government-led delivery to shared value creation
examples.exemplar: MaRS Discovery District convenes public, private, and social actors.

examples.label: From transactions to trust-based alliances
examples.exemplar: Collective Impact emphasizes shared measurement and backbone support.

designProvocation: What outcome is impossible alone—but plausible together?
